constraints.coverage_assumption: User wants a curated, broad set of influential LLM-agent papers; I will treat "important" as corpus-relative and methodological-salient within the evaluation surveys, and clearly flag external recommendations as beyond-corpus.

entities: tool use, planning, memory, multi-agent dimensions
normalized_question: Recommend important papers on LLM-based agents, grounded in the available corpus (survey on LLM-based agent evaluation, and survey on evaluating LLM-based agents for multi-turn conversations), while acknowledging that the corpus itself is limited to evaluation-focused surveys and broader influential LLM-agent papers are outside the inventory.

obligations.description: What sub-area of 'LLM-based agents' the recommendations should cover (e.g., evaluation, multi-turn dialogue, tool use, planning, memory, multi-agent systems).
Whether each recommended paper is in the user-visible corpus (the two evaluation surveys) or only available as external knowledge, with reason grounded in evidence.

Whether the available corpus of two evaluation surveys is sufficient to ground 'important papers on LLM-based agents' as a general literature recommendation, vs. a narrower evaluation-focused recommendation.

raw_question: Recommend some important papers on LLM-based agents.
